brain_consensus.narrative: Fear has a stranglehold on the market, with sentiment plunging to extreme lows not seen since the August 2024 bottom, yet stablecoin reserves are quietly swelling on the sidelines. This identical fear-and-liquidity signature preceded a sharp relief rally last summer, as sidelined capital rushed in to absorb the panic-driven discount. The market is coiling for a similar resolution, with the only question being whether the trapdoor opens before the spring.
brain_consensus.headline: Extreme Fear Meets Silent Liquidity Buildup

next_move.reasoning: Despite the 78.5% match to bottom_2024_aug Disbelief, the current score sits in Anxiety (39-46) not Disbelief (55-62), and massive divergences in ETF flows (+45), funding (+19), and VIX (+28) show this is NOT a true bottom analog—it's a mid-cycle chop where momentum is elevated but sentiment remains fearful, creating conflicting signals that historically resolved as sideways grind; my 0% direction accuracy demands humility here.
